business.headline: Axis Bank appoints ex-Bandhan Bank finance chief Rajeev Mantri as CFOMantri will assume charge on September 28, 2026, subject to the necessary regulatory and statutory approvals, the bank said in a regulatory filing.

JPMorgan just posted record profit. How big is the world's biggest bank, really?JPMorgan Chase posted a record second-quarter 2026 profit as revenue rose across every business line. The results renewed focus on the bank's vast scale, from market value to daily payment flows.

India may get Rs 10 and 20 plastic currency notes from next year. Here's whyThe Reserve Bank of India has begun preparations for a pilot of polymer banknotes by inviting global bids for substrate supply. The trial, likely to start with Rs 10 and Rs 20 notes, will shape any wider rollout.

SpiceJet fined Rs 1 lakh for misleading consent tactics, loyalty programme opt-insA consumer watchdog finds SpiceJet's booking interface nudged users into loyalty enrolment and promotional consent, raising fresh questions over how digital platforms secure customer approval.
Reliance Q1 profit falls 22% despite 25% jump in revenue. Here's whyReliance Industries posted a 22% drop in first-quarter net profit even as revenue rose sharply.

Founder's life is hard: $1.2 billion fintech CEO says bank rejected home loanDespite a long relationship with the lender, a strong credit score and significant assets, a fintech founder said a leading private sector bank rejected his home loan application.

Apple beats Nvidia to become world's most valuable companyApple's market capitalisation stood at around $4.88 trillion on Friday as its shares remained largely steady. Nvidia, meanwhile, slipped to around $4.86 trillion after its shares fell 3.5%.

Awareness, better policies can unlock India's tourism potential, say expertsSpeaking at the India Today's FAITH Tourism Conclave 2026, tourism industry representatives said India should focus on promoting year-round travel, creating greater awareness about unique destinations and ensuring stable policies to support the sector.

When no asset looks an easy bet, do multi-asset allocation funds make sense?Equity markets have faced a sharp correction amid geopolitical tensions, while gold and silver, traditionally considered safe havens during periods of uncertainty, have also fallen sharply from the all-time highs they touched in January.

Fake bosses, real losses: Sebi warns of scammers posing as CEOs in rising boss scamSebi has warned companies about a rising 'boss scam' in which fraudsters impersonate senior executives to trick employees into transferring funds, and urged firms to strengthen their security mechanisms and enhance vigilance.

F1 could return to India in 2028 as government races to fix old tax hurdlesThe government has begun talks with stakeholders to revive Formula One in India by 2028. The push centres on fixing old tax and regulatory hurdles that derailed the Indian Grand Prix.

VFS Global continues to invest in AI to streamline visa application processingSpeaking at India Today's FAITH Tourism Conclave 2026, Yummi Talwar, COO, South Asia, VFS Global, said visa demand has largely returned to normal after the post-pandemic surge.

Sensex ends nearly 1,000 points up: 3 reasons why stock market jumped todaySensex and Nifty surged on Friday as IT stocks, Reliance Industries and private banks led buying. The S&P BSE Sensex was up 953.76 points, or 1.24%, at 78,140.63 around 3:07 pm, after touching an intraday high of 78,282.55.

8th Pay Commission: How much HRA could you get under different fitment factors?The HRA debate comes at an important stage in the 8th Pay Commission process. It has been more than seven months since the Commission was constituted. The Commission visited Kolkata, West Bengal, on July 9 and 10, 2026, as part of its ongoing consultation exercise.

Your ITR could be wrong even with Form 16: 7 smart checks to avoid tax noticesThink your Form 16 is all you need to file your ITR? Think again. A few missed details could delay your refund, or even land you a tax notice.

Maruti challenges landmark E20 ruling, says contaminated fuel was the real issueMaruti Suzuki will challenge the Raipur consumer court order on a Grand Vitara E20 dispute. The case sharpens scrutiny of whether alleged damage came from ethanol-blended petrol or contaminated fuel.

Why investors are betting against Elon Musk's SpaceXSome investors are concerned about the company's heavy investment in artificial intelligence, much of which is being financed through debt. These concerns have added pressure on the stock in recent weeks.

EPFO plans PF scheme for self-employed and unorganised workersIf implemented, the proposed scheme could significantly expand India's social security system by giving millions of self-employed individuals and workers in the unorganised sector an opportunity to save for retirement through a trusted and structured provident fund mechanism.

Trump posts, markets move. His company now wants to turn that into a businessTrump Media plans to launch Truth PSI, a paid service offering early access to Truth Social posts. The proposal has prompted criticism over possible market advantage and conflict-of-interest concerns.

Infosys, TCS, Tech Mahindra, HCLTech rally 2%; Why are IT stocks rising today?Tech Mahindra's stronger June-quarter earnings lifted IT shares, with Infosys, TCS and HCLTech joining the rally while Wipro fell. The divergent moves highlighted improving confidence in stronger performers as investors watched the sector's earnings season.

entertainment.headline: The Odyssey box office Day 3: Nolan's film scripts biggest opening weekend in India Director Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey crossed Rs 70 crore gross in India during its opening weekend. The three-day run also made it the director's biggest opener in the country.

This is unity: Tom Cruise's stirring FIFA World Cup speech leaves fans emotional Tom Cruise opened the FIFA World Cup 2026 final with a speech on football's power to unite people. His address set the tone for a showpiece night that ended with Spain beating Argentina after extra time.

Shakira to BTS: FIFA's World Cup final halftime show a celebration of global pop FIFA's first World Cup final halftime show brought Madonna, BTS, Justin Bieber, Shakira, Burna Boy and Coldplay together in an 11-minute set. Fans quickly latched on to Jungkook and Shakira's shared moments as the tournament unveiled a new musical spectacle.

FIFA 2026: Mohanlal shares video from New York, gives special message to fans Actor Mohanlal attended the FIFA World Cup 2026 final in New York and shared a video from the stadium before Spain faced Argentina. His post captured the pre-match mood as Spain went on to beat Argentina 1-0.

Salman Khan shares cryptic post after his recent appearance sparks health concerns Actor Salman Khan's appearance at the Slum Rehabilitation Authority's new support centre in Mumbai prompted questions about his health. He later shared a cryptic post, addressing the online speculation.

Akshay Kumar, Saif Ali Khan and other celebs attend IND vs ENG ODI at Lord's. Pics Several Bollywood stars, including Akshay Kumar, Saif Ali Khan and Taimur Ali Khan, were among the notable faces seen at the iconic London cricket ground. The celebrity appearances added an extra highlight to the India vs England ODI, with pictures from the stands gaining attention online.

How are they contributing?: Anuradha Paudwal's LGBTQ+ remarks trigger backlash Singer Anuradha Paudwal sparked controversy after questioning how the LGBTQ+ community contributes to society during a recent podcast. Her remarks have drawn criticism from several social media users.

Shabana Azmi meets CJP protesters ahead of Sansad march; Prakash Raj joins. Watch Shabana Azmi and Prakash Raj joined the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) protest at Jantar Mantar ahead of its planned Parliament march. Videos of the actors expressing solidarity with the protesters have gone viral on social media.

Tanishk Bagchi claims royalty dues for Saiyaara track in deleted post; YRF reacts A royalty dispute has emerged between composer Tanishk Bagchi and Yash Raj Films over the hit Saiyaara title track. While Tanishk claimed that Rs 8 lakh in royalties remain unpaid, YRF said all payments have been made as per the agreed contract.

Singer Jasmine Sandlas mobbed at Dehradun concert; video sparks outrage A distressing video has surfaced from Dehradun showing Indian-American singer Jasmine Sandlas being mobbed, with fans alleging she was touched inappropriately while attempting to accept flowers.

Journey that led you to it: RGV praises Yami Gautam after National Award win Ram Gopal Varma praised Yami Gautam after she won the Best Actress award for Article 370 at the 72nd National Film Awards. The filmmaker highlighted her dedication, perseverance and the journey that led to the prestigious recognition.

CM Vijay's Jana Nayagan advance bookings open in India; shows sell out at Rs 2500 Advance bookings for Vijay's Jana Nayagan have opened in India, with several shows already selling out. The response remains strong despite premium pricing in Bengaluru and follows months of delay and certification issues.

Nick Jonas wishes Priyanka Chopra on her birthday with loved-up Spain vacation clip Singer Nick Jonas celebrated actor Priyanka Chopra's 44th birthday with a romantic video from their Spain vacation and a sweet message on Instagram. The heartfelt post came as the actor also unveiled her first look from SS Rajamouli's Varanasi on her birthday.

Yash blushes as fans scream 'Daddy's home' at Ramayana trailer launch. Watch Actor Yash joined his co-actors Ranbir Kapoor and Sai Pallavi at the Ramayana trailer launch as fans erupted in Daddy's home chants. The rousing welcome put the spotlight firmly on his arrival.

Is he unwell?: Salman Khan's latest Mumbai appearance sparks concern among fans Salman Khan attended the inauguration of the Slum Rehabilitation Authority's new support centre in Mumbai on Friday night. Videos from the event later sparked concern online, with some fans saying the actor looked ill.

Heartstopper Forever review: A comforting farewell that doesn't quite take risksRelease Date:Fri Jul 17, 2026 Heartstopper Forever delivers an emotionally sincere farewell that stays true to the series' hopeful spirit, even if it occasionally plays things too safe. The feature-length finale values kindness over conflict, making for a comforting but imperfect goodbye.

Jaaved Jaaferi recalls how Boogie Woogie was almost rejected as 'down market' Actor Jaaved Jaaferi recalled how Boogie Woogie was initially rejected by Sony's creative team despite a strong pilot. He said the show finally got the green light because then Sony executive Sudesh Iyer believed in the idea, along with the remarkable talent of children from Mumbai's suburbs.

Today is the moment for me: Yami Gautam shares note after National Award win Actor Yami Gautam reflected on her 14-year journey in Hindi cinema after winning the National Film Award for Best Actress for Article 370. Calling the honour a dream come true, she thanked her family, team and audiences while describing the film as one of the most personal projects of her career.

Samay Raina urges fans to stop trolling Sakshi Jha after viral Latent episode Stand-up comedian Samay Raina asked viewers to be kind to Sakshi Jha after her India's Got Latent remarks triggered backlash. His appeal came as memes, trolling and arguments over feminism kept the controversy alive online.

general news.headline: Sonam Wangchuk says he will end hunger strike today, but 3 conditions applyA note listing the three conditions was posted on Sonam Wangchuk's X handle on Monday, July 20, the date of CJP's planned march to Parliament. He said he would break the fast that day itself if any of the three demands were met.

Trump orders more strikes as another US soldier killed in Iranian attack on IraqCENTCOM said it launched a fresh wave of air strikes against Iran for the ninth consecutive night. The United States said the operation is meant to weaken assets linked to attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

5,000 personnel, water cannons: Delhi Police's plan for CJP Parliament marchAs the CJP prepares for its 'Chalo Sansad' march on the opening day of Parliament's Monsoon Session, the Delhi Police has mounted a massive security operation, deploying over 5,000 personnel and placing the high-security district under heightened surveillance.

JD Vance, wife Usha welcome fourth child. Here's what they named himAccording to reports, the birth is historically significant as it marks the first time in more than 150 years that a sitting US vice president has welcomed a child while in office.

Trouble for Pak on four fronts. Is a new nation on the horizon?From Balochistan and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa to POK and the Afghan border, Pakistan is facing four distinct but increasingly interconnected crises. Together, they reveal the erosion of state authority and the limits of Asim Munir's security-first approach. A former Afghan leader suggests that a new country might be taking shape.

Video: Karnataka man falls 40 feet after zipline snaps, suffers severe injuriesA 24-year-old Karnataka man is battling severe injuries after a 30–40-foot fall during a zipline ride at a Dandeli resort.

Paper leaks, Ram Temple row: Govt, Opposition ready for Monsoon session showdown Live UpdatesParliament's Monsoon Session begins amid heightened political tensions, with the Opposition set to raise issues including the NEET paper leak, Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike and the Ram Temple donation row, while the NDA seeks to leverage its strengthened numbers to push key legislation.

Salman Khan shares cryptic post after his recent appearance sparks health concernsActor Salman Khan's appearance at the Slum Rehabilitation Authority's new support centre in Mumbai prompted questions about his health. He later shared a cryptic post, addressing the online speculation.

Water's greatest mystery solved? India-born physicist's science-defying discoveryHandcrafted ByOur Editors For over a century, electric sparks fired into water have produced explosions stronger than science can explain. An 84-year-old Indian-origin physicist claims he has finally solved the mystery, and says the answer points to clean energy from ordinary water.

Monsoon set for a strong comeback: When and where will it rain this week?The monsoon is set for a strong comeback early this week, with widespread rain expected over North, East and Central India. A fresh Bay of Bengal low-pressure system will pull the monsoon trough back into position and end the sticky heat.

Video: Girl's school fight ends with her family tracking down, attacking 2 sistersA minor school dispute takes a violent turn in Gujarat when a family allegedly tracks down two sisters on their way to tuition, stops their scooter and launches a brutal road attack.

Threw condoms at me: Hyderabad IPS trainee accuses batchmate of assault, stalkingAccording to the complaint lodged on July 18, the complainant, Suma HK, a 30-year-old IPS probationer undergoing training at the Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel National Police Academy (SVPNPA), alleged that fellow trainee Uday Krishna Reddy had subjected her to sustained harassment over the past few weeks.

Inside Ramayana Delhi launch: Ranbir as Ram, Yash's grandeur and Sunny's surpriseThe Ramayana team gathered in Delhi for the trailer launch and Pratham Sankalp ceremony. The event brought the principal cast together as the makers outlined the film's wider cultural and theatrical plans.

Live: Large gatherings banned in central Delhi ahead of CJP's protest marchCJP Protest in Delhi Live Updates: After Sonam Wangchuk gets hospitalised, Delhi Police have deployed personnel and are closely monitoring the situation at Jantar Mantar as the Cockroach Janta Party prepares for its proposed march towards Parliament. Security has been tightened to maintain law and order and prevent any untoward incident.

Lionel Messi hugs Lamine Yamal, passes the torch after Spain's World Cup winLionel Messi and Lamine Yamal shared an emotional embrace after Spain beat Argentina 1-0 after extra time to win the FIFA World Cup.

Patient killed after ceiling fan falls on him, hospital blames medical conditionA patient died hours after a ceiling fan allegedly fell on his bed in Delhi's GTB Hospital emergency ward. The incident has triggered demands for an inquiry into maintenance and patient safety lapses.

The Odyssey box office Day 3: Nolan's film scripts biggest opening weekend in IndiaDirector Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey crossed Rs 70 crore gross in India during its opening weekend. The three-day run also made it the director's biggest opener in the country.

India's rain has paused, but its deadliest monsoon killer is out. What is it?The IMD has forecast thunderstorms with lightning across nearly every region of India this week, even as monsoon rain stays subdued. Lightning kills more Indians than any other weather hazard, and dry, muggy spells are exactly when it strikes.

51 in maths, IIT, ISRO and rockets: The story of Pawan Kumar ChandanaSaturday was a big day for India's space ambitions. The launch of Vikram-I this morning was successful. Now, the man behind the launch, an IITian, a former ISRO scientist, and an ambitious entrepreneur who gave India's space journey a new direction, has emerged from the ground to become a sensation and an inspiration for many youngsters.

US Command recovers unidentified remains in Jordan; identification underwayCENTCOM said troops recovered unidentified remains from the site of the Iranian attack in Jordan during a Sunday search. The remains are being examined to establish whether they are those of the missing US service member, while identities remain withheld pending family notification.

India's top economists say skip college, but India still wants 50% in itHandcrafted ByOur Editors Several of India's top government advisers now tell students to skip college, even as youth unemployment hits 16 percent and IT layoffs mount. But the "skip college" push has a hole: Musk's Tesla still hires mostly degree-holders, and India's own policy targets 50 percent college enrolment by 2035 — the opposite ask.

Know your laws: Wangchuk case raises a bigger question on hunger strikesFrom Irom Sharmila to Sonam Wangchuk, India's history of hunger strikes meets a new legal question: when does a protest become attempted suicide, and how far can the State go to intervene?

Chennai's Amma canteens see 55% surge in footfall after CM Vijay's revamp pushDaily footfall at Chennai's 383 Amma Canteens has risen from 67,164 in April to over 1.04 lakh after the Tamil Nadu government launched a renovation and modernisation drive.

15-hour workdays, 4 months without pay: Hyderabad woman seeks rescue from OmanShabnam Begum from Hyderabad has sought help from the Indian Embassy in Muscat after alleging abuse by her employer in Oman. Her family has sought her safe return and action against the agent who allegedly sent her on false promises.

sports.headline: Shoaib Akhtar enters Messi-Ronaldo GOAT debate. Cricket fans ask why Shoaib Akhtar used a World Cup final panel to argue that Cristiano Ronaldo is football's biggest brand but Lionel Messi the better player. But the cricket fans asked a simple question. Why was the Pakistan legend present on the panel of a football show?

Argentina coach breaks down while reflecting on World Cup final defeat: Watch Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni broke down during his post-match press conference after Spain ended his side's World Cup title defence. Scaloni praised Lionel Messi and his players while admitting the defeat "hurts our souls" after the 1-0 final loss.

World Cup was written in the stars for Spain: Ferran Torres after beating Argentina Ferran Torres said Spain's World Cup triumph over Argentina was "written in the stars" after scoring the extra-time winner in the final. The forward admitted Lionel Messi always brings nerves but hailed Spain's commitment to their style of football.

FIFA World Cup awards: Did Rodri deserve Golden Ball over Messi? FIFA World Cup 2026: Rodri won the Golden Ball after captaining Spain to a 1-0 extra-time win over Argentina in the World Cup final. The award stirred immediate debate as many felt Lionel Messi's eight-goal campaign deserved the top individual honour.

Argentina player punches Spanish star on the face after World Cup final loss Leandro Paredes clashed with two Spanish footballers after Argentina's embarrassing loss in the FIFA World Cup final. This happened moments after the final whistle was blown and Spain were crowned as the world champions.

Trump refuses to leave podium in awkward moment as Spain lift World Cup: Video US President Donald Trump was briefly asked to move aside during Spain's World Cup trophy celebrations after presenting the trophy to captain Rodri. The moment quickly went viral as officials cleared the stage for Spain's players to lift football's

Lionel Messi moved to tears after FIFA World Cup Final heartbreak Lionel Messi was overcome with emotion after Argentina's FIFA World Cup title defence ended in heartbreak. Messi broke down in tears as Spain defeated the defending champions 1-0 in the Extra Time to lift their second FIFA World Cup title.

Lionel Messi hugs Lamine Yamal, passes the torch after Spain's World Cup win Lionel Messi and Lamine Yamal shared an emotional embrace after Spain beat Argentina 1-0 after extra time to win the FIFA World Cup.

Spain make Messi's Argentina look toothless to win 2nd World Cup FIFA World Cup Final: Spain wore down Argentina before Ferran Torres scored the extra-time winner at MetLife Stadium. The goal ended Lionel Messi's title defence and delivered Spain a second World Cup crown.

Spain fans rage after VAR chalks off Nico Williams' World Cup final goal World Cup final: Spain were left raging after Nico Williams' goal in the World Cup final against Argentina was ruled out by VAR for a foul in the build-up. The controversial decision sparked a social media storm, with many fans insisting the contact was too minimal to cancel the goal.

World Cup final: Lisandro Martinez's fiery display ends in injury, sparks frenzy among Man United fans Argentina defender Lisandro Martinez's combative World Cup final came to a painful end after he was forced off injured moments after receiving the first yellow card of the match. The setback sparked concern among Manchester United fans ahead of the new season.

Shubman Gill breaks silence on Rohit retirement rumours after Lord's hundredIndia vs England: India captain Shubman Gill dismissed speculation over Rohit Sharma's ODI future after the defeat to England at Lord's, saying there had been no discussion within the team about the veteran opener's retirement despite reports linking the 39-year-old with an international exit after the England series.

Rohit Sharma 138 not enough as England clinch series 2-1 at Lord'sRohit Sharma's magnificent 138 was not enough as England overcame fighting half-centuries from Shubman Gill (77) and Virat Kohli (74) to beat India by 27 runs in the third ODI at Lord's on Sunday and seal the series 2-1.

No helmet off, no celebration: Rohit Sharma lets his Lord's hundred do the talkingIndia vs England: Rohit Sharma answered the retirement rumours in the best way he knows, with a magnificent century at Lord's on Sunday that kept India alive in their daunting chase of 388 against England in the series-deciding third ODI.

ESP 1-0 ARG Highlights: Heartbreak for Messi as Spain win 2nd World CupFIFA World Cup Final Highlights, Spain vs Argentina (ESP 1-0 ARG): Argentina arrived as champions but barely laid a glove on Spain, whose relentless control was finally rewarded with a second World Cup crown.

Rohit Sharma, 39 years young, sends Lord's crowd wild with electric diving effortRohit Sharma turned back the clock at Lord's, producing a stunning diving stop and a crucial catch to delight the crowd during the third ODI against England. The fielding masterclass came amid speculation surrounding the Indian captain's ODI future.

Ben Duckett breaks Viv Richards' 47-year-old Lord's ODI record with 141 vs IndiaEngland opener Ben Duckett scored a superb 141 against India at Lord's on Sunday to break Viv Richards' 47-year-old record for the highest individual ODI score at the venue.

Kuldeep Yadav's confidence could take a hit after repeated ODI snub: AshwinRavichandran Ashwin questioned India's decision to leave Kuldeep Yadav out of the playing XI for the third ODI against England at Lord's, saying repeated omissions could disrupt the wrist-spinner's rhythm and dent his confidence.

Order in the court of ball: Football's ties to law and beyondFootball pitches mirror courtrooms in their reliance on strict, equal rules. Yet, behind the sporting brilliance, both systems are increasingly manipulated by corporate avarice and administrative overreach.

Messi might win the World Cup again, but this prized trophy could still elude himMbappe's brace against England sent him clear of Messi in the Golden Boot race before Sunday's final. Messi must now break down Spain's stern defence to seize the award outright.

American boxer Hannah Rapp dies in fatal cycle crash, car driver arrestedAmerican boxer Hannah Rapp, 26, died after being struck by a car while cycling in Texas, with authorities arresting a 31-year-old driver on manslaughter charges.

Rift? What rift? Virat Kohli and Gambhir share a laugh in viral photo in LondonEngland vs India: Virat Kohli and Gautam Gambhir share a warm laugh at the Indian High Commission, followed by crucial boundary-line talks at Lord's, proving the rumoured dressing-room rift is entirely nonexistent.

India, England players observe minute's silence at Lord's to remember SobersIndia and England players observed a minute's silence and wore black armbands before the Lord's ODI as a mark of respect for Sir Garfield Sobers. The West Indies legend passed away on 17th July.

Zimbabwe vs Bangladesh, 3rd T20I: Highlights from BulawayoZIM vs BAN, 3rd T20I Highlights: Zimbabwe have won the toss and opted to bat first in the series decider in Bulawayo. Abdul Gaffar Saqlain has replaced the injured Nahid Rana for Bangladesh.

Jasprit Bumrah misses crucial Lord's ODIs against England. Here's whyIndia have been dealt a huge blow ahead of the series-deciding third ODI at Lord's, with Jasprit Bumrah ruled out due to reactive swelling in his left knee. The visitors, chasing their first ODI win at the venue since 2004, have made three changes to the playing XI.

World Cup final live streaming: All you need to know about Argentina vs SpainArgentina face Spain in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final at MetLife Stadium. Messi's charge for redemption meets Yamal's bid to own the game's biggest stage. Here's all you need to know about the livestreaming details of the clash.

Bangladesh sweat on Nahid Rana's fitness before high-stakes Australia TestsBangladesh have been dealt a major injury scare ahead of their first Test tour of Australia in 18 years, with pace sensation Nahid Rana battling a side strain sustained during the Zimbabwe T20I series.

Is it done? PV Sindhu silences doubters with flawless Japan Open triumphAfter ending a 19-month title drought in Tokyo, PV Sindhu reflects on silencing doubters, trusting her attacking game, and rediscovering the self-belief that has reignited her World Championships hopes.

Spanish Renaissance: How De la Fuente modernised tiki-taka to revive SpainFIFA World Cup 2026 final: Spain stands on the cusp of history as their renaissance in World football is about to come to a full circle. Standing behind the turnaround is Luis de La Fuente, a manager who re-engineered La Roja's tiki-taka DNA, and man-managed his players to bring out their best.

3rd ODI highlights: Rohit 138 in vain as England win Lord's ODI to seal series 2-1India (IND) vs England (ENG), 3rd ODI highlights: Rohit Sharma's superb 138 was not enough as England sealed the three-match ODI series 2-1 with a victory in the decider at Lord's on Sunday. Chasing 388 after England piled up 387 for 3, India remained in contention for long thanks to Rohit's counter-attacking century, but Sam Curran's timely breakthroughs derailed the chase as the hosts held their nerve to complete the win.

LAKR win maiden MLC title to leave Shah Rukh Khan ecstatic. Here's what he saidLos Angeles Knight Riders beat Washington Freedom by one run to win their first Major League Cricket title. This left Shah Rukh Khan elated as the Knight Riders owner praised his side for their title triumph.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
